concerns.text: FR-005 states Cue‑Retrieval Efficiency is computed 'relative to chance level' but does not define what 'chance level' means (e.g., 1/N_agents, random baseline, etc.). This makes the metric unverifiable.
FR-003 specifies a 'standardized <MEMORY_ACTION> token' but does not define the exact format/schema. Without a concrete format, read/write operations cannot be deterministically verified.
FR-007 references 'Methodological Soundness – Multiplicity' section which does not exist in the spec. Broken internal reference.
FR-008 references 'Threshold Justification & Sensitivity' section which does not exist in the spec. Broken internal reference.
FR-009 references 'Multiplicity & Power' section which does not exist in the spec. Broken internal reference.
SC-003 requires a 'monotonic trend report' but does not specify the expected direction (increasing or decreasing with token count). Monotonicity alone is ambiguous for verification.
Edge Cases section lists questions rather than verifiable requirements. These should be converted to FRs or SCs (e.g., 'System MUST handle malformed tokens by logging and continuing').
